query: WGA health fund $321 million studio contribution premium increase 2026 details

mako.raw:
{"query":"WGA health fund $321 million studio contribution premium increase 2026 details","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1697,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://variety.com/2026/film/news/wga-deal-321-million-health-fund-residuals-ai-1236711731/","title":"WGA Deal: $321 Million for Health Fund, Better Residuals, AI Meetings","content":"The health plan changes are the centerpiece of the four-year contract reached by the WGA and the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers over the weekend.
“Our most significant accomplishment was restoring our health plan to a sustainable path after facing severe pressure from industry contraction and runaway healthcare cost inflation.”
They also agreed to raise their health contributions from 13% of a writer’s gross compensation to 16.75%.
The studios also agreed to raise the earnings caps used to calculate those contributions.
Writers currently pay zero monthly premiums for individual coverage, and $50 for a family.
Deductibles will increase from $400 to $500 for individuals, and from $1,200 to $1,500 for a family."},{"url":"https://www.wga.org/contracts/contracts/mba/summary-of-the-2026-wga-mba","title":"Summary of the 2026 WGA MBA","content":"The Health Fund contribution on reportable earnings will increase 3.25% in the first year of the agreement, from 13% to 16.25%, and then another 0.5% in the second year, to 16.75%.
To address rising out-of-network expenses, the Fund will be adjusting its reimbursement of out-of-network behavioral healthcare to be in line with reimbursement for all other out-of-network care.
For comparison, the Mercer Benchmark1 estimates that typical premiums for employer-sponsored healthcare are $185 per month for single coverage and $682 per month for a family.
The contract increases the annual funding for the Showrunner Training Program from $250,000 to $275,000, which will support both WGAW and WGAE’s programs.
2More details on the Centivo Partnership Plan will be available later in 2026."},{"url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/business-news/writers-contract-deal-321m-health-plan-infusion-1236556126/","title":"WGA Contract Deal Contains $321M Health Plan Infusion","content":"The WGA West board and the WGA East council unanimously approved the agreement when they met on Tuesday.
The deal will additionally funnel $25 million from the union’s Paid Parental Leave fund to the health fund.
The WGA told members in its summary of the agreement that the Paid Parental Leave fund “will still maintain sufficient funding to pay out benefits.”
The most successful streaming platforms (Netflix, Amazon and Disney+), meanwhile, will pay more in foreign residuals to writers."},{"url":"https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/business/story/2026-04-09/writers-guild-studios-wga-health-care-hollywood-labor-unions-new-contract","title":"Writers Guild earned a historic healthcare increase in studio deal - Los Angeles Times","content":"The major Hollywood studios will pitch in $320 million to sustain the health fund of the Writers Guild of America, the union told its members Wednesday.
The pending contract was first approved last weekend and the boards of WGA East and West approved the deal on Tuesday.
The earnings threshold to get coverage would increase about $7,000 to $53,773."},{"url":"https://deadline.com/2026/04/wga-2026-contract-details-studios-1236784944/","title":"WGA Reveals Details Of Its Studio Deal","content":"After securing the confidence of their boards of directors, the Writers Guild of America East and West on Wednesday revealed the details of the new tentative agreement with the studios and streamers, including the plan to reestablish the solvency of the union health plan.
The Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers has agreed to pay a record $321 million into the WGA health plan.
If ratified later this month, the 2026 minimum basic agreement will span four years — a concession that is sure to ruffle feathers with the other above-the-line unions.
That vote will run April 16-24."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"WGA health fund $321 million studio contribution premium increase 2026 details","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://variety.com/2026/film/news/wga-deal-321-million-health-fund-residuals-ai-1236711731","title":"WGA Deal: $321 Million for Health Fund, Better Residuals ...","content":"They also agreed to raise their health contributions from 13% of a writer's gross compensation to 16.75%. increases in premiums, deductibles.","score":0.85421735,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/business-news/writers-contract-deal-321m-health-plan-infusion-1236556126","title":"WGA Contract Deal Contains $321M Health Plan Infusion","content":"The tentative contract also raises minimum payments by a cumulative 10.5 percent and sweetens the union’s streaming success bonus. The Writers Guild of America on Wednesday disclosed the details of the surprise four-year deal leaders reached with studios and streamers the previous weekend, revealing that the tentative contract contains a $321 million infusion into the union’s ailing health plan, higher foreign and domestic residuals and language covering licensing of work for AI training**.**. The deal also raises minimum payments by 10.5 percent over the course of four years, sweetens the union’s streaming success bonus and increases second-step payments for screenwriters. The agreement also raises the health fund contribution on reported earnings 3.25 percent in the first year of the deal and 0.5 percent in the second year of the deal, by which time the contribution rate will be 16.75 percent. The majority of minimum payments to writers will increase by 1.5 percent in the first year of the contract and 3 percent in the second, third and fourth years.","score":0.7944651,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.wgacontract.org/the-campaign/summary-of-the-2026-wga-mba","title":"Summary of the 2026 WGA MBA","content":"Most MBA minimums increase by 1.5% for the first year of the agreement, and will increase another 3% on May 2, 2027, 3% on May 2, 2028, and 3% on May 2, 2029. * **Screen:** The Health Fund contribution cap on screen projects will increase for the first year of the MBA from $250,000 to $325,000, then to $375,000 as of May 2, 2027, and to $400,000 as of May 2, 2028. * **Pilots:** The Health Fund contribution cap on pilots will increase for the first year of the MBA from $225,000 for a one-hour and $170,000 for a half-hour to $270,000 for a one-hour and $200,000 for a half-hour in the first year of the MBA, and then to $300,000 for a one-hour and $225,000 for a half-hour as of May 2, 2028. * **Overall Deals:** The Health Fund contribution cap on overall deals will increase for the first year of the MBA from $275,000 to $325,000, to $375,000 as of May 2, 2027, and to $400,000 as of May 2, 2028.","score":0.7680558,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.wga.org/contracts/contracts/mba/2026-mba-contract-changes-faq","title":"2026 MBA Contract Changes FAQ","content":"The Health Fund contribution on reportable earnings will increase 3.25% in the first year of the agreement, from 13% to 16.25%, and then another 0.5% in the","score":0.75831425,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/business/story/2026-04-09/writers-guild-studios-wga-health-care-hollywood-labor-unions-new-contract","title":"WGA deal includes $320 million to shore up health plan","content":"WGA deal includes $320 million to shore up health plan ; DGA ratifies four-year contract with major studios. June 26, 2026 ; DGA's board backs","score":0.6757865,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.9,"request_id":"eefc58b7-63c4-47d0-922d-7e92ba3062d0"}
